codeReview.summary: Implementation satisfies the feature contract: project-scope rules remain language-filtered, user-scope rules remain unfiltered/global, and no-language project scope now emits an explicit warning and performs a successful no-op in both `rules -y -n` and `install -y -n` flows.

issues.description: Test name says user scope 'still installs all rules', but the assertion only checks absence of language/warning output and does not assert that the full rule set is actually staged/output. This weakens regression protection for VAL-SCOPE-007 completeness.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `cli-refactor-worker` mandates running `bun run test:unit`, `bun run test:integration:mock`, and `bun run test:integration:real`, but the reviewed worker used `bun test cli/tests/` as a combined run and still reported `followedProcedure: true`. Skill guidance could explicitly allow equivalent aggregate commands to avoid ambiguity.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill file `/var/home/milton/agentfiles/.factory/skills/cli-refactor-worker/SKILL.md` (Work Procedure step 6) vs transcript skeleton entry showing `Execute: bun test cli/tests/` for session `ce1c0b04-fb42-4661-8a41-a05a052bde35`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered that integration tests spawning the CLI from non-repo cwd must use an absolute CLI script path; relative `cli/src/cli.ts` fails under cwd-switched fixtures. This testing pitfall is not currently captured in `.factory/library/`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton includes debug output `error: Module not found "cli/src/cli.ts"` followed by test fix to use `const CLI_SCRIPT = CWD + "/cli/src/cli.ts";` in `cli/tests/integration/real/rules-language-policy.test.ts`.

summary: Reviewed handoff, commit diff, transcript skeleton, skill procedure, and mission shared-state docs. The feature is functionally correct and meets VAL-SCOPE-006/007/008 plus VAL-CROSS-008. One non-blocking automated-test assertion gap was found, plus two shared-state observations (skill-procedure clarity and reusable test-harness knowledge).
